name: 2026-05-17 Business & SME Advocacy SME Finance Paradox TH
description:
Eighty-nine percent of Filipino small businesses expect to grow in 2026. That's the highest of any market in Asia-Pacific.

The same regional survey ranked the Philippines dead last in access to finance.

Hindi tayo dapat sanay sa mediocre.

The builders are doing the work. The financial system still won't bet on them.

custom_fields.value:
Eighty-nine percent of Filipino small businesses expect to grow in 2026. That's the highest of any market in Asia-Pacific.

The same regional survey ranked the Philippines dead last in access to finance.

Hindi tayo dapat sanay sa mediocre.

The builders are doing the work. The financial system still won't bet on them.

Eighty-nine percent of Filipino small businesses expect to grow in 2026. That's the highest of any market in Asia-Pacific.

The same regional survey ranked the Philippines dead last in access to finance.

Hindi tayo dapat sanay sa mediocre.

The builders are doing the work. The financial system still won't bet on them.

CPA Australia 17th Annual Asia-Pacific Small Business Survey released April 2026 (fieldwork Nov-Dec 2025; 4,166 small businesses across 11 Asia-Pacific markets). Findings: Philippines ranked last of 11 markets for ease of accessing finance, even as 89% of PH small businesses expect to grow in 2026 (highest expectation regionally) and 79% reported 2025 growth (4th highest in the region; strongest PH result since 2019). Reported by PR Newswire APAC, The Manila Times, Inquirer Business.
